column1: # policy-gate-dependencies.tsv — install-time copy manifest for policy-gate workflows.
# install-policy-gates.sh copies every dependency row for a workflow it installs,
# so an installed target project's CI checkout has the script/data files that
# workflow's `bash scripts/enforcement/...` (or similar) steps actually call.
# test-install-policy-gate-coverage.sh fails closed if a workflow's real
# `bash scripts/enforcement/...` call site has no matching row here, so a new
# gate dependency is a manifest row, not a forgotten copy block.
